n    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)\\n    cosine_steps = max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)\\n    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.2, total_iters=warmup_steps)\\n    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_steps)\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(\\n        optimizer,\\n        schedulers=[warmup, cosine],\\n        milestones=[warmup_steps],\\n    )\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Under a fixed 256-step budget, the baseline is likely under-optimized: it uses a conservative Adam LR and an effectively inert scheduler.
